title: ONT Library Prep for Whole Genome Sequencing

guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4h567","text":"Longer incubation with AMPure beads leads to higher yield.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: End-prep Reaction
Clean-Up
Prepare the following:

In a 1.5 mL tube, mix the following for each sample: Item Volume (μL) 1 DNA CS (optional) 1 2 DNA sample 47 3 Ultra II End-prep Reaction Buffer 3.5 4 Ultra II End-prep Enzyme Mix 3 5 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Buffer 3.5 6 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix 2 If you do not use DNA CS, add 1 µL of Nuclease-free water instead.
Thoroughly mix by pipetting using a wide-bore tip and avoid creating bubbles.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 on bench top and 65 °C for 00:05:00 on thermomixer.
Vortex AMPure XP Beads (AXP).
Add 60 µL of the resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the end-prep reaction(s).
Incubate tube(s) on the Hula mixer 10 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Spin down the tube(s) and pellet on a magnet.
Keep the tube(s) on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant.
Keep the tube(s) on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol without disturbing the pellet.
Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard without disturbing the pellet.
Keep the tube(s) on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol without disturbing the pellet (again).
Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard without disturbing the pellet (again).
Allow to dry for ~30 seconds, but do NOT dry the pellet to the point of cracking.
Remove tube(s) from magnet and resuspend the pellet in 61 µL Nuclease-free water .
Incubate tube(s) for minimum 00:10:00 at 37 °C .
Pellet the beads on a magnet.
Remove and retain 60 µL of eluate into a new 1.5 mL tube(s).
Adapter ligation reaction

In the same 1.5 mL tube, mix in the following order for each sample: Item Volume (μL) 1 DNA sample (from the previous step) 60 2 Ligation Adapter (LA) 5 3 Ligation Buffer (LNB) 25 4 NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 10
For each sample: Adjust 1.4 µg of DNA volume to 47 µL with Nuclease-free water in a 1.5 mL tube and mix using a wide-bore tip.
Prepare fresh 80% ethanol for the wash steps. Each sample will need 400 μL.
Thoroughly mix by gently pipetting using a wide-bore tip and avoid creating bubbles.
Incubate tube(s) at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Clean-up
Vortex AMPure XP Beads (AXP).
Add 40 µL of the resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction(s).
Incubate tube(s) on the Hula mixer at 10 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Spin down the tube(s) and pellet on a magnet.
Keep the tube(s) on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant.
Remove tube(s) from magnet and wash the beads by adding 250 µL Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) .
Resuspend the beads by pipetting with wide-bore tip.
Pellet the beads on a magnet and pipette off the supernatant.
Remove tube(s) from magnet and wash the beads by adding 250 µL Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) (again).
Resuspend the beads by pipetting with wide-bore tip (again).
Pellet the beads on a magnet and pipette off the supernatant (again).
Allow to dry for ~30 seconds, but do NOT dry the pellet to the point of cracking.
Remove tube(s) from magnet and resuspend the pellet in 32 µL Elution Buffer (EB) .
Incubate for minimum 00:10:00 at 37 °C
Pellet the beads on a magnet.
Remove and retain 32 µL of eluate into a new 1.5 mL tube(s).
Qubit each library and keep on ice until loading or store in -20°C.
Set two heat blocks to 65 °C and 37 °C .
creator: Jasmine Sakr
